frameworks.name: recurrent neural networks
frameworks.one_line: RNNs trained without connectivity constraints used to model neural computation.

frameworks.name: low-rank / low-dimensional connectivity
frameworks.one_line: Extends prior constrained low-rank RNN theory to unconstrained trained networks.

frameworks.name: continual learning / catastrophic forgetting
frameworks.one_line: Cited as motivation for separating functions across weight subspaces.

core_question: How does network dynamics relate to connectivity in RNNs trained without specific structural constraints?
key_assumptions: removing connectivity components that strongly affect local dynamics identifies functionally relevant structure
task performance is a valid readout of functional sufficiency of a weight subspace
manipulations: identification and removal of operative dimensions from the weight matrix
reconstruction of RNNs from only a few operative dimensions
tasks previously employed in neuroscience used as training objectives
key_findings: a low-dimensional, functionally relevant subspace exists within apparently high-dimensional trained connectivity
weight matrices built from only a few operative dimensions sustain original RNN performance
much of the high-dimensional structure of trained connectivity is functionally irrelevant
interpretations: an operative low-dimensional subspace simplifies linking connectivity to dynamics in unconstrained RNNs
independent network functions could be placed in separate weight subspaces to avoid catastrophic forgetting
